questions.question: Who is eligible to connect Linear to Notion AI?

options.explanation: Only a Linear workspace admin can initiate the connection.
The Notion workspace must have an owner perform the connection, not just any member.

options.text: Only workspaces on the Personal plan
options.explanation: The workspace must be on the Business or Enterprise plan.

options.text: All of the above conditions must be met
options.explanation: You need to be a Linear admin, a Notion owner, and be on a Business/Enterprise plan.

questions.question: What is the maximum time the initial Linear‑Notion AI integration can take to complete?

options.explanation: The article states the process can take longer than 12 hours.
While possible, the upper limit mentioned is higher.
The integration can take up to 36 hours depending on workspace size.
48 hours exceeds the maximum time described.

questions.question: Does Notion AI ingest attachments and documents from Linear?

options.text: Yes, all file types are imported
options.explanation: The article explicitly says attachments are not ingested.

options.text: No, attachments are not ingested at this time
options.explanation: Currently, Notion AI does not read attachments or documents from Linear.
No file type is imported right now.
Images are also not imported according to the article.

questions.question: Which type of Linear data is NOT currently supported for Notion AI to read?

options.explanation: Issue titles are included in the data Notion AI can read.
Comments are part of the accessible data.
Project progress (and milestones) are explicitly not supported yet.
Assignee details are included in the accessible data.

questions.question: How frequently does Notion AI sync permission changes between Linear and Notion?

options.explanation: The sync interval is longer than 15 minutes.
Permission changes are synced hourly, as stated in the article.
Sync occurs more often than once a day.
The system does not provide real‑time syncing; it runs hourly.

questions.question: After the integration is set up, for how far back can Notion AI reference issue updates?

options.explanation: The retention window is longer than 30 days.
The article specifies a one‑year look‑back.
Notion AI can reference issues updated within the past year.
Only the most recent year is accessible.

questions.question: Can a user who is a Linear member but NOT a Notion workspace member use the integration?

options.text: Yes, they can read data but not write
options.explanation: Both memberships are required to use the integration.

options.text: No, they cannot use the integration
options.explanation: The integration is limited to individuals who belong to both workspaces.

options.text: Only if they are a Linear admin
options.explanation: Admin status alone does not bypass the Notion membership requirement.
Access is blocked entirely without Notion membership.

questions.question: Where can a Linear admin revoke the Notion AI connector’s access?

options.text: In Notion AI settings under Linear
options.explanation: Admins revoke from Linear, not from Notion.
Admins go to Settings → Applications → select the app → Revoke access.

options.text: In the Notion workspace settings page
options.explanation: Revocation for admins is performed in Linear, not Notion.
The correct path is through Applications, not team settings.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT part of the official Linear‑Notion AI connection workflow?

options.text: Navigate to Settings → Notion AI → Linear in Notion and click Linear
options.explanation: This is the first step in the documented process.

options.text: Authorize Linear to access your team when redirected
options.explanation: Granting authorization is required after the redirect.

options.text: Manually upload a CSV of issues to Notion
options.explanation: The workflow never mentions uploading a CSV; data sync is automatic.

options.text: If the redirect fails, click “Check connection” in Notion to retry
options.explanation: The article instructs users to use “Check connection” after a failed redirect.
